ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) ProxyLogon webshell execution — w3wp.exe spawning shell / recon / dump tools
ucs.description: Detects the canonical Silk Typhoon post-exploitation pattern on Microsoft Exchange (CVE-2021-26855/26857/26858/27065 'ProxyLogon' chain): the IIS Exchange worker process w3wp.exe spawning shells, recon binaries, archivers, or LSASS-dumping tooling — i.e. China-Chopper-style .aspx webshells executing OS commands under the Exchange app pool identity. Also covers the renewed 2024-2025 Silk Typhoon edge-device exploitation pivot.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" AND Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","schtasks.exe","tasklist.exe","ntdsutil.exe","procdump.exe","procdump64.exe","rundll32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","7z.exe","7za.exe","rar.exe","makecab.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.process_hash | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | search parent_process IN ("*MSExchange*","*OWA*","*ECP*","*HttpProxy*","*RpcProxy*") OR (parent_process="*w3wp*" AND process_name IN ("procdump.exe","procdump64.exe","ntdsutil.exe")) | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) — Exchange w3wp child shell / recon / credential-dump
// Maps to MSTIC-published HAFNIUM ProxyLogon post-exploitation IOCs
let ExchangeAppPool = dynamic(["MSExchange","OWA","ECP","HttpProxy","RpcProxy","PowerShellAppPool"]);
let SuspiciousChildren = dynamic([
    "cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe",
    "whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe",
    "schtasks.exe","tasklist.exe","ntdsutil.exe","procdump.exe","procdump64.exe",
    "rundll32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe",
    "7z.exe","7za.exe","rar.exe","makecab.exe","sqlcmd.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
| where InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any (ExchangeAppPool)        // w3wp under an Exchange app pool
| where FileName in~ (SuspiciousChildren)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"                                       // exclude machine accounts
| extend IsLsassDump = (FileName in~ ("procdump.exe","procdump64.exe") and ProcessCommandLine has "lsass")
         or (FileName =~ "rundll32.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "comsvcs.dll" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("MiniDump","#24"))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ChildFile   = FileName,
          ChildCmd    = ProcessCommandLine,
          IsLsassDump,
          SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) — Exchange w3wp child shell on Sentinel via SecurityEvent 4688
// Requires GPO 'Include command line in process creation events' enabled on the Exchange host
let SuspiciousChildren = dynamic([
    "cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe",
    "whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe",
    "schtasks.exe","tasklist.exe","ntdsutil.exe","procdump.exe","procdump64.exe",
    "rundll32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe",
    "7z.exe","7za.exe","rar.exe","makecab.exe","sqlcmd.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where ParentProcessName endswith "\\w3wp.exe"
| extend ChildName = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ChildName in (SuspiciousChildren)
| where AccountType == "User" and Account !endswith "$"
| extend IsLsassDump = (ChildName in ("procdump.exe","procdump64.exe") and CommandLine has "lsass")
         or (ChildName == "rundll32.exe" and CommandLine has "comsvcs.dll" and (CommandLine has "MiniDump" or CommandLine has "#24"))
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine, IsLsassDump, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) Exchange w3wp Child Shell / Credential Dump
id: 6f7f0c4b-2d5b-4d6f-9b71-silktyphoon01
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects the canonical HAFNIUM/Silk Typhoon ProxyLogon post-exploitation pattern
  where the IIS Exchange worker process w3wp.exe (running under an Exchange app pool)
  spawns shells, host-recon binaries, archivers, or LSASS-dumping utilities — i.e.
  China-Chopper .aspx webshells executing OS commands.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2021/03/02/hafnium-targeting-exchange-servers/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0125/
author: SOC Detection Team
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.credential_access
  - attack.t1003.001
  - attack.g0125
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: process_creation
detection:
  parent_w3wp:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\w3wp.exe'
  exchange_apppool:
    ParentCommandLine|contains:
      - 'MSExchange'
      - 'OWA'
      - 'ECP'
      - 'HttpProxy'
      - 'RpcProxy'
  suspicious_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
      - '\quser.exe'
      - '\ntdsutil.exe'
      - '\procdump.exe'
      - '\procdump64.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\7z.exe'
      - '\rar.exe'
      - '\makecab.exe'
      - '\schtasks.exe'
  condition: parent_w3wp and (exchange_apppool or suspicious_child)
falsepositives:
  - Exchange admin running diagnostic shells from inside an OWA/ECP request (rare)
  - Authorised vulnerability scanners testing Exchange
level: high
ucs.rationale: This is the single most actor-defining behaviour for Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM. Microsoft's MSTIC publication on the ProxyLogon zero-day chain (CVE-2021-26855 SSRF + CVE-2021-27065 arbitrary write) explicitly documents the post-exploit pattern: a webshell dropped under \inetpub or \Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\* gets executed by w3wp.exe inside an Exchange app pool, which then spawns cmd/po
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) LSASS credential dump via ProcDump / comsvcs MiniDump / PowerShell reflection
ucs.description: Hunts the specific credential-access tooling Microsoft attributed to HAFNIUM during the 2021 Exchange campaigns and again in 2023-2025: procdump64.exe -ma lsass.exe, rundll32 comsvcs.dll MiniDump #24 against the LSASS PID, and PowerShell MiniDumpWriteDump reflection. Used by Silk Typhoon after webshell foothold to extract domain credentials before lateral movement.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("procdump.exe","procdump64.exe") AND Processes.process="*lsass*") OR (Processes.process_name="rundll32.exe" AND Processes.process="*comsvcs.dll*" AND (Processes.process="*MiniDump*" OR Processes.process="*#24*")) OR (Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") AND Processes.process="*MiniDumpWriteDump*" AND Processes.process="*lsass*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.process_hash | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) — LSASS credential extraction via procdump / comsvcs / PS reflection
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where (FileName in~ ("procdump.exe","procdump64.exe")
            and ProcessCommandLine has "lsass")
      or (FileName =~ "rundll32.exe"
            and ProcessCommandLine has "comsvcs.dll"
            and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("MiniDump","#24"))
      or (FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
            and ProcessCommandLine has "MiniDumpWriteDump"
            and ProcessCommandLine has "lsass")
| extend Method = case(
    FileName has "procdump", "ProcDump -ma lsass",
    FileName =~ "rundll32.exe", "comsvcs.dll MiniDump",
    "PowerShell MiniDumpWriteDump")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, Method,
          FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          ParentFile = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd  = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          IsParentW3wp = (InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"),
          SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) — LSASS credential extraction (Sentinel SecurityEvent 4688)
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where AccountType == "User" and Account !endswith "$"
| extend ChildName = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where (ChildName in ("procdump.exe","procdump64.exe") and CommandLine has "lsass")
     or (ChildName == "rundll32.exe" and CommandLine has "comsvcs.dll" and (CommandLine has "MiniDump" or CommandLine has "#24"))
     or (ChildName in ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") and CommandLine has "MiniDumpWriteDump" and CommandLine has "lsass")
| extend Method = case(
    ChildName has "procdump", "ProcDump -ma lsass",
    ChildName == "rundll32.exe", "comsvcs.dll MiniDump",
    "PowerShell MiniDumpWriteDump")
| extend IsParentW3wp = ParentProcessName endswith "\\w3wp.exe"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, Method, ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine, IsParentW3wp, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) LSASS Credential Dump Tooling
id: 6f7f0c4b-2d5b-4d6f-9b71-silktyphoon02
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects the LSASS credential-access tooling Microsoft attributed to HAFNIUM /
  Silk Typhoon during ProxyLogon and follow-on campaigns: procdump64.exe -ma lsass,
  rundll32 comsvcs.dll MiniDump #24, and PowerShell MiniDumpWriteDump reflection.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2021/03/02/hafnium-targeting-exchange-servers/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0125/
author: SOC Detection Team
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.credential_access
  - attack.t1003.001
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1218.011
  - attack.g0125
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: process_creation
detection:
  procdump_lsass:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\procdump.exe'
      - '\procdump64.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: 'lsass'
  comsvcs_minidump:
    Image|endswith: '\rundll32.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'comsvcs.dll'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'MiniDump'
      - '#24'
  powershell_minidump:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'MiniDumpWriteDump'
      - 'lsass'
  condition: procdump_lsass or comsvcs_minidump or powershell_minidump
falsepositives:
  - Authorised IR / forensics tooling capturing LSASS for triage
level: high
ucs.rationale: Microsoft's HAFNIUM advisory specifically named ProcDump against LSASS as the credential-extraction step that followed ProxyLogon webshell deployment, and CISA's AA21-062A reproduced the exact procdump.exe -accepteula -ma lsass.exe command-line as a HAFNIUM IOC. The comsvcs.dll MiniDump variant (`rundll32 C:\Windows\System32\comsvcs.dll MiniDump <pid> <out> full`) is the LOLBin alternative the gro
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
